case.description: Availability requests should return a concrete stub summary and availability workflow trace.
Image-only requests should route through image intake and explain the stub limitation clearly.

actual.summary: Received your screenshot. Image-driven scheduling is ready in OpenAI mode.

assertions.name: summary_contains
assertions.expected: Image-driven scheduling is ready in OpenAI mode
assertions.actual: Received your screenshot. Image-driven scheduling is ready in OpenAI mode.

case.description: Overslept requests should use the day reorganization workflow in stub mode.

actual.summary: Got it. Reorganize routine items by 45 minutes (preserving meetings and sleep).

assertions.name: summary_contains
assertions.actual: Got it. Reorganize routine items by 45 minutes (preserving meetings and sleep).

case.description: Dry-run undo should preview the action without executing it.

actual.summary: Dry run: would undo your most recent change.

assertions.name: summary_contains
assertions.actual: Dry run: would undo your most recent change.

case.description: A guard return should stop execution when search results are ambiguous.
A successful plan should search, move, and return a done payload with op ids.
Tool failures should surface as execution errors with the failing tool in the trace.
ToolHost should create an event and return the op id plus event id.
ToolHost should return summarized search results from the calendar client.
ToolHost should reject write tools in dry-run mode before executing them.

actual.error: ValueError: Tool calendar.create is not available in dry-run mode

assertions.name: error_contains
assertions.actual: ValueError: Tool calendar.create is not available in dry-run mode

case.description: ToolHost should project free and busy windows into the contract shape.
ToolHost should persist a scheduling rule through the policy store.
ToolHost should serialize task list items through the task service.
Availability questions should classify into the lookup workflow and stay read-only.

actual.rationale: The request is asking for open time or scheduling feasibility.

workflow_definition.description: Inspect free time or confirm when the user is available.
planner_notes: Answer with concrete windows or constraints.
Stay read-only unless the user explicitly asks for a change.
Use calendar.search or calendar.list before discussing a specific event.

case.description: Dry-run requests should keep the event workflow but clear the write expectation.

actual.rationale: The request appears to target a calendar event or new calendar block.

workflow_definition.description: Create, inspect, move, update, or delete calendar events.
planner_notes: Lookup before mutating when the event is not already unambiguous.
Use freebusy before proposing or creating a new time block.
If multiple plausible events match, stop and ask one short clarification.

case.description: Moving an existing event should classify into event management and require lookup before mutation.

actual.rationale: The request appears to target a calendar event or new calendar block.

workflow_definition.description: Create, inspect, move, update, or delete calendar events.
planner_notes: Lookup before mutating when the event is not already unambiguous.
Use freebusy before proposing or creating a new time block.
If multiple plausible events match, stop and ask one short clarification.

case.description: Image-based scheduling intake should select the image workflow.

actual.rationale: An image-only request needs extraction before a scheduling action.

workflow_definition.description: Extract scheduling details from an image, then plan the right calendar or task action.
planner_notes: Extract the relevant scheduling facts from the image before deciding on tools.
Clarify only if the image is ambiguous enough that acting would be risky.

case.description: Durable scheduling rules should go through policy management.

actual.rationale: The request sounds like a durable planning rule or habit.

workflow_definition.description: Persist or inspect durable scheduling rules.
planner_notes: Use policies for durable habits or rules that should apply later.
Reflect the saved rule back to the user in plain language.

case.description: Overslept or delay requests should use the day reorganization workflow.

actual.rationale: The request is about shifting the rest of the day after a disruption.

workflow_definition.description: Repair the rest of the day after a delay, oversleep, or disruption.
planner_notes: Use the dedicated day reorganization tool when the request is about a delay or oversleep.
Preserve fixed meetings and user constraints.

case.description: Task scheduling requests should route through task management with calendar support.

actual.rationale: The request uses task-oriented language or refers to due work.

workflow_definition.description: Manage task-like items and optionally place them on the calendar.
planner_notes: Treat the task list as the source of truth for task edits.
Use freebusy before scheduling task blocks unless timing is explicit.
When scheduling from a task, preserve the link between the task and the work block.

case.description: Undo requests should map cleanly to the undo workflow.

actual.rationale: The user is explicitly asking to reverse a prior operation.

workflow_definition.description: Inspect recent operations or undo one.
planner_notes: Prefer undo when the user clearly wants to reverse a recent change.
Use history first only if the user refers to an older or ambiguous operation.
